description_long: This is a pair of old-fashioned, forged metal scissors with a dark, weathered appearance, likely made from iron or steel. The handles are simple, forming two oval loops that are roughly symmetrical and unadorned, with no visible padding or ergonomic shaping. The blades are long, tapering to a sharp, pointed tip, and show signs of wear and possible rust, indicating extensive use or age. There is no visible pivot screw or rivet, suggesting a fixed, non-adjustable joint typical of early scissor designs. The overall construction is utilitarian, with a rough, matte finish and no decorative elements. No brand markings or manufacturer information are visible on any part of the object.
description: A pair of old, forged metal scissors with oval handles and long, pointed blades, showing signs of wear and a rough, matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the scissors, highlighting the elongated blades and oval handles.
description_view_1: Angled view emphasizing the pointed blade tips and the thickness of the metal construction.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full length and the symmetry of the handles and blades.
description_view_3: View from the handle end, clearly displaying the two oval finger loops and the alignment of the blades.
attribute_handle_type: Symmetrical oval loops, uncoated, not ergonomic, ambidextrous, no special grip features.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Fixed joint, likely riveted, not visibly adjustable, no distinctive design features.
attribute_finger_loop_design: Two equal-sized oval loops, plain metal, no inserts or contours, accommodates standard finger placement.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Pointed blade tips, designed for precision cutting, not rounded or blunted.
